summary: The cornea receives sensory innervation primarily via the long ciliary nerves, which originate from the ophthalmic division (V1) of the trigeminal nerve. These nerves emerge from the nasociliary nerve, making option D—which contains an apparent typo for "nasolacrimal"—correctly point toward this pathway when interpreted accurately.
